question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConsensusMode.EQUAL] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the runtime configuration switch consensusMode from STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK to EQUAL, causing all nodes to be weighted equally (stake=1) so that a minority of colluding nodes can reach the 1/3 consensus threshold and suppress legitimate transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConsensusMode.STAKE] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a switch to ConsensusMode.STAKE (which includes nodes outside the address book in total stake calculation), artificially inflating totalStake so that legitimate nodes can never accumulate 1/3 of stake, effectively preventing any transaction gossip from reaching consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a network name value containing the NETWORK_PREFIX_DELIMITER '-' such that getNetwork() returns an unexpected substring, causing the importer to resolve the wrong cloud bucket and download no stream files, preventing all transaction gossip from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a network string like 'mainnet-' (trailing delimiter) to make getNetworkPrefix() return null instead of a valid prefix, causing the stream file provider to fail to discover the correct network folder and silently skip all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileData whose filename is fewer than 10 characters, causing getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() to throw a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException via substring(0, 10), crashing the downloader and preventing all subsequent transaction gossip from being archived?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user inject path-traversal sequences (e.g., '../../') into the first 10 characters of a stream filename so that getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() resolves to an unintended directory, overwriting critical files and preventing legitimate transaction gossip from being stored?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filename whose first 10 characters contain null bytes or special filesystem characters, causing getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() to produce an invalid path that silently discards all archived transaction gossip files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nodePublicKey] Can an unprivileged external user who can set nodePublicKey to a value they control cause AddressBookServiceImpl.getNodes() to override every node's public key with that single key, so that NodeSignatureVerifier fails to verify any legitimate node signature, preventing all transaction gossip from passing consensus validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nodePublicKey] Can an unprivileged external user set nodePublicKey to an empty string or whitespace, bypassing the StringUtils.isNotBlank() guard in AddressBookServiceImpl.getNodes() and leaving node public keys as null, causing NodeSignatureVerifier to log a warning and skip all signature verification, allowing forged gossip to pass?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nodePublicKey] Can an unprivileged external user set nodePublicKey to a syntactically valid but cryptographically invalid RSA public key string, causing NodeSignatureVerifier to throw an exception for every node signature and preventing all legitimate transaction gossip from being verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: endDate] Can an unprivileged external user set endDate to a past Instant (e.g., Instant.EPOCH), causing DateRangeCalculator.getFilter() to produce a DateRangeFilter where end < start, so that every transaction timestamp fails the filter and no transaction gossip is ever processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: endDate] Can an unprivileged external user set endDate to a value just before the current consensus timestamp, causing Downloader.verifySignatures() to call downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) and permanently halt all transaction gossip downloading for the lifetime of the process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: startDate] Can an unprivileged external user set startDate to a far-future Instant, causing DateRangeCalculator.getLastStreamFile() to throw an InvalidConfigurationException (effective startDate > endDate), crashing the downloader startup and preventing any transaction gossip from being ingested?
